content.text: Rollout succeeded. The note warns an external process may change state, so let me re-verify the live replica count and the Ray node count.

input.command:
export KUBECONFIG=${KUBECONFIG:-~/.kube/config}
echo "=== deploy spec/status ==="
kubectl -n ray get deploy ray-worker -o jsonpath='spec.replicas={.spec.replicas} ready={.status.readyReplicas} avail={.status.availableReplicas}{"\n"}'
echo "=== ray status ==="
kubectl -n ray exec ray-head-86d4fc5c85-mb4md -- ray status 2>&1 | grep -iE "node_|Active|Healthy|node$" | head -30

content.text: The deployment is solidly at 3/3 ready. The Ray "7 active nodes" includes stale entries — a worker pod is still `Terminating` and the `ray-client` pod also registers as a node. Let me wait for it to settle and re-verify stability (guarding against the external process).

input.command:
export KUBECONFIG=${KUBECONFIG:-~/.kube/config}
for i in 1 2 3; do
  sleep 15
  spec=$(kubectl -n ray get deploy ray-worker -o jsonpath='{.spec.replicas}')
  ready=$(kubectl -n ray get deploy ray-worker -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}')
  running=$(kubectl -n ray get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/component=worker --field-selector=status.phase=Running --no-headers 2>/dev/null | grep -c Running)
  echo "check $i: spec=$spec ready=$ready running_worker_pods=$running"
done
echo "=== ray nodes count ==="
kubectl -n ray exec ray-head-86d4fc5c85-mb4md -- ray status 2>&1 | sed -n '/Active:/,/Pending:/p' | grep -c "1 node_"
